businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and enterprise AI data analytics platform — transforming from US defense/intelligence core partner into a commercial AI powerhouse
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds software platforms that analyze and integrate complex data. AIP (AI Platform) has driven explosive commercial growth. Q4 2025 revenue $1.407B (+70% YoY), adjusted operating margins 57%. Closed at $152.42 (+6.14%) on 3/6, as Peter Thiel met Japan PM Sanae Takaichi in Tokyo to discuss AI cooperation. Strong V-shaped rebound from prior day's -9.92% crash. Rallied +6% despite broad macro selloff (jobs shock + oil $90+). P/E 246x, P/S 78x.
howTheyMakeMoney: Government contracts: US DoD, CIA, NSA — software licensing and maintenance
Commercial: AIP-based enterprise AI analytics platform subscriptions — US commercial revenue +137% YoY
International government: NATO allies and Five Eyes partner contracts
keyProducts: AIP (AI Platform): Connects LLMs to enterprise data for AI-driven analytics
Gotham: Government/defense data analysis and decision support
Foundry: Enterprise data integration and analytics platform
Apollo: Software deployment and management automation
Maven: US DoD AI operational support system

keyMetrics.name: P/E Ratio (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme valuation persists. Rebound pushed multiples back up

keyMetrics.name: P/S Ratio (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Among the highest revenue multiples in software

keyMetrics.name: Adj. Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Exceptional profitability for a software platform

growthDrivers.title: Peter Thiel Meets Japan PM Sanae Takaichi in Tokyo
growthDrivers.description: Benzinga: Thiel discussed AI and technology cooperation with PM Takaichi on 3/6. Palantir Japan expansion expected. Japan government AI contracts possible.

growthDrivers.title: +6.14% Counter-Market Rally — Solo Strength Amid Macro Selloff
growthDrivers.description: While Nasdaq plunged on jobs shock + oil surge, PLTR alone rallied +6%. V-shaped recovery from prior -9.92% crash.

growthDrivers.title: AIP Bootcamp Cementing Enterprise AI Dominance
growthDrivers.description: FinancialContent: AIP bootcamp strategy accelerating enterprise customer conversion. Expanding into healthcare and automotive sectors.

growthDrivers.title: Seeking Alpha: 'Revenue +70%, Margins 57% — Fundamentals Intact Despite Thiel Selling'
growthDrivers.description: Peter Thiel sold 2M shares ($290M) on 3/2, but revenue growth and margin indicators remain strong.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: Government security clearances (Top Secret/SCI), 20-year defense/intelligence relationships, and AIP enterprise AI platform expansion are the core moats.

moats.description: Top Secret/SCI certified. Extremely difficult for competitors to enter.
Gotham/Foundry deeply integrated into customer data infrastructure. Very high replacement costs.
AIP bootcamps rapidly expanding enterprise customer base. More usage → better models.

competitors.name: Snowflake
competitors.detail: Data cloud platform. Enterprise analytics competition

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Azure AI services targeting government and enterprise

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Much smaller scale vs PLTR

recentNews.significance: Palantir Japan expansion expected. Deepening US-Japan tech alliance opens government AI contracts

recentNews.headline: PLTR +6.14% counter-market rally amid broad selloff
recentNews.significance: V-shaped recovery from -9.92%. Counter-market +6% signals strong institutional buying

recentNews.significance: Insider selling headlines vs fundamental strength. Positive signal for long-term investors

recentNews.significance: AIP strategy spreading to new verticals. Data privacy concerns noted

recentNews.significance: 38% pullback renewing valuation debate. Growth sustainability is key question

analystOpinions.notableComment: 18 analysts, Buy consensus. Rebound narrows upside to +27% (from +35% prior day). Rosenblatt $200, Citigroup $255 high targets. Japan expansion news could catalyze further upgrades.

risks.title: Extreme valuation — P/E 246x, P/S 78x
risks.description: Rebound expanded valuation burden. Growth deceleration would trigger severe correction.

risks.title: Insider mega-selling — Peter Thiel $290M
risks.description: Thiel sold 2M shares ($290M) on 3/2. Co-founder mass selling weighs on sentiment.

risks.title: Geopolitical two-way risk
risks.description: Iran war escalation → defense beneficiary, peace talks → sharp selloff. Extreme volatility.

risks.title: Commercial competition intensifying
risks.description: Snowflake, Databricks, Microsoft Azure AI competing for enterprise AI market share.

risks.title: SBC dilution
risks.description: Annual 4.67% share count increase from stock-based compensation.

buyReasons.title: Counter-market +6% rally — strong institutional buying signal
buyReasons.rationale: Solo +6% on a day when the entire market crashed. Deep institutional conviction.

buyReasons.title: Japan PM meeting → Asia government AI expansion
buyReasons.rationale: Thiel-Takaichi meeting opens Japan government AI contract potential. Revenue diversification.

buyReasons.title: US commercial revenue +137% YoY explosion
buyReasons.rationale: AIP bootcamp accelerating enterprise customer conversion. Growth engine beyond defense.

buyReasons.title: Net cash $6.95B — financial fortress
buyReasons.rationale: Zero debt, abundant cash. Downside risk limited.

overallOpinion: V-shaped +6.14% rebound from prior day's -9.92% crash, closing at $152.42. Defied the broad macro selloff driven by jobs shock and oil surge.
Peter Thiel met Japan's PM in Tokyo to discuss AI cooperation. Palantir Japan expansion and government AI contract expectations drove the rally.
P/E 246x, P/S 78x remain extreme. Thiel's $290M selling is a concern, but revenue +70% and 57% margins confirm fundamentals are intact.
Testing 50-day SMA at $155. 200-day at $165 is mid-term resistance. RSI 48.5 neutral, recovering from oversold territory.
